title: India Property Developer K Raheja Said to Delay $700 Million IPO
summary: K Raheja Corp. Pvt., one of India’s largest real estate developers, has delayed plans for an initial public offering after feedback from investment bankers, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Samsung Record Shows Earnings Beats Often Trigger Profit-Taking
summary: A selloff in Samsung Electronics Co. shares Tuesday, despite stronger-than-expected preliminary earnings, underscored the tendency of the stock’s investors to cash out as the market often prices in its milestones ahead of time.

title: US Trade Deficit Widens to Biggest in More Than a Year
summary: The US trade deficit widened in May as a broad-based increase in imports and a decline in exports propelled the gap to the most in more than a year.

title: Apple Supplier Luxshare Prices $3.1 Billion HK Listing at Top
summary: Apple Inc. supplier Luxshare Precision Industry Co. raised HK$24.3 billion ($3.1 billion) after pricing shares for its Hong Kong listing at the maximum amount it had set.

summary: A group of hedge fund veterans is helping drive a fundamental shift in how to generate financing for developing nations.

title: Oil Creeps Higher as Fresh Ship Attacks Underscore Hormuz Risks
summary: Oil nudged higher following attacks on shipping in and around the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting continued risks to vessels in the critical waterway.

title: Italy’s Oldest Dairy Taps New Way to Borrow Against Cheese
summary: Italy’s oldest dairy company has borrowed money against cheese aging in its warehouse, taking advantage of a law change aimed at helping food and wine producers access finance.

summary: Urals prices back where they were before US, Israel launched campaign against Iran

title: Dangote Says Kenya Refinery Will Cost as Much as $17 Billion
summary: A new mega-refinery to be built at the Kenyan coast by Africa’s richest person will cost as much as $17 billion, a spokesman for Dangote Industries Ltd. has confirmed.

title: InsuranceDekho Said to Pick Banks for $400 Million India IPO
summary: Indian insurance aggregator InsuranceDekho’s parent has selected HSBC Holdings Plc, Morgan Stanley, ICICI Securities Ltd. and IIFL Capital Services Ltd. as advisers to a potential initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: US Stocks Fall as Chipmakers Tumble and Oil Climbs: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks fell as volatility hit chipmakers anew after blowout earnings from Samsung Electronics Co. left investors wanting even more. Bonds slipped as Brent crude headed for the biggest gain in more than a week.

title: France Unveils New Cuts as Weaker Growth Imperils Deficit Goal
summary: France said it would make €3 billion ($3.4 billion) of additional budget cuts this year as it struggles to keep on track with narrowing the budget shortfall after weaker economic activity threatened its plans.

title: Goldman Says Earnings to Drive Next Leg of Rally in HALO Stocks
summary: Capital-intensive companies are likely to report solid earnings this season, further outperforming peers that are more reliant on human or digital assets, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc. strategists.

title: Amazon Seeks to Raise at Least $25 Billion From Bond Sale
summary: Amazon.com Inc. is looking to raise at least $25 billion from a US dollar bond sale, its latest funding push as the company ramps up investment in artificial intelligence infrastructure.

title: Equinor’s Chief Crude Trader Leaves After Nine-Year Tenure
summary: Equinor ASA’s chief crude trader, David Barratt, left the company this week following a string of other departures from the Norwegian energy giant.

title: Standard Nuclear Seeks to Raise $383 Million in US IPO
summary: Standard Nuclear Inc., a maker of fuel for nuclear reactors, is seeking to raise as much as $383 million in a US initial public offering.

title: Gas Demand Set for First Drop Since 2022 on Iran War, IEA Says
summary: Global gas demand is poised for the first annual drop since the 2022 energy crisis because of higher prices caused by the Iran war, according to the International Energy Agency.

title: Autodoc Is Raising €530 Million Loan to Buy Out Apollo’s Stake
summary: German online car-parts retailer Autodoc Holding SE is raising €530 million ($606 million) in debt financing to help it buy back a minority stake held by Apollo Global Management Inc.

title: Accounting Firm CBIZ Urged by Activist Investor to Pursue M&A
summary: A CBIZ Inc. shareholder is urging the accounting firm to re-evaluate its capital allocation, abandon a share buyback plan and return to pursuing acquisitions, according to a letter sent to the company.

title: Dudley: Still Bullish on AI Spending
summary: Katrina Dudley, Franklin Templeton Senior Investment Strategist, discusses the slump in Samsung shares and why she continues to be bullish on AI infrastructure spending. She speaks with Bloomberg's Vonnie Quinn on “Bloomberg Brief.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Samsung Earnings Send Chip Stocks Tumbling as Kospi Triggers Circuit Breaker | The Pulse 7/7/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Jim Reid, Deutsche Bank Research Institute Head and Global Head of Macro and Thematic Research; Marta Prochwicz, European Council on Foreign Relations Deputy Head of the Warsaw office; Pierroberto Folgiero, Fincantieri CEO    (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Arrives in Turkey With Iran War at Top of Agenda
summary: President Donald Trump lands in Ankara, Turkey for the NATO summit where he will start the day visiting with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. Bloomberg's Annmarie Hordern is on the ground with the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Premarket Movers for July 7, 2026
summary: S&P 500 Index futures slip 0.2% as of 7:41 a.m. in New York. Volatility hit chipmakers after Samsung Electronics Co.’s blowout earnings left investors wanting more.

title: US Stocks Fall as Samsung Results Fail to Impress | Bloomberg Brief 7/07/2026
summary: US equity futures slide as global chipmakers drag following Samsung's earnings results. The world's largest chipmaker by market value failed to impress investors even with its 19-fold profit surge. Oil jumps as fresh attacks on ships in and around the Strait of Hormuz raise concerns about the US-Iran deal. NATO Summit kicks off in Ankara, Turkey. Katrina Dudley of Franklin Templeton discusses tech valuations.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Tech Volatility Hits Highest Since Dot-Com Bust Next to S&P 500
summary: The higher the rally in technology high-flyers, the louder the anxiety around a new wave of turbulence in the group.

title: Tech Weakness Resumes After Samsung Misses Lofty AI Expectations
summary: US stock futures pointed to renewed weakness in technology shares after Samsung Electronics Co.’s earnings failed to satisfy investors, renewing pressure on chipmakers across global markets.

title: Fincantieri Says Hybrid Warfare Is Growing Threat to Europe Seas
summary: Hybrid warfare presents an emerging threat to Europe’s underwater cable and energy links, the chief executive officer of Italian shipbuilder Fincantieri SpA said.

title: Saudi-Flagged Crude Oil Tanker Sustained Damage in Hormuz Strait
summary: A crude oil tanker sailing under the flag of Saudi Arabia sustained damage while navigating the Strait of Hormuz, a person with knowledge of the matter said, the latest incident to highlight that navigation through the waterway remains risky.

title: Le Pen Judgement Day, Samsung Drops after Profit Beat | The Opening Trade 7/7/2026
summary:
The outcome of Marine Le Pen’s appeal of her conviction for embezzling public funds could shape French and European politics for years. The decision due today from the three-judge appellate panel will determine not only whether the 57-year-old three-time presidential candidate gets another shot at the Élysée, but whether Jordan Bardella, her 30-year-old protégé, becomes the National Rally’s face in what polls suggest could be the far-right party’s best opportunity yet to win power.

The chip sector came under more pressure, with Samsung Electronics, whose shares have more than doubled this year, plunging as much as 10% despite reporting a 19-fold jump in profit. 

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards and Guy Johnson. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Falls as Traders Weigh Fed Rate Path and Hormuz Attacks
summary: Gold extended a pullback from a two-week high, as traders weighed the outlook for US interest rates and renewed attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
